home.heroSubtitle: I design the model architectures of tomorrow — bio-inspired, world-model-oriented and efficient beyond Transformers, across application domains. Currently a Mitacs Globalink visiting researcher at the University of Alberta's Vision & Learning Lab (physics-grounded 3D motion generation). My aim: to become a Research Scientist at Google.

notable: Mitacs Globalink research excellence scholarship: visiting researcher at the University of Alberta's Vision & Learning Lab (physics-grounded 3D motion generation)

Ranked among the top 3 (3/93) of ENSEIRB-MATMECA's computer-science track, and winner of a national innovation competition with EcoSort
Vice-president of ENSEIRB-MATMECA's Ingenib recruitment forum (team of 22)

about.title: About Me
about.intro: An engineering student at ENSEIRB-MATMECA (engineer-doctor track), I devote my research to designing the model architectures of tomorrow — bio-inspired, world-model-oriented and efficient beyond Transformers. I'm currently a Mitacs Globalink visiting researcher at the University of Alberta's Vision & Learning Lab, working on physics-grounded 3D motion generation. My goal: a Research Scientist role at Google.
about.section1Title: An Unconventional Journey Driven by Passion
about.section1p1: My passion for computing began very early. At 12 years old, I discovered Scratch; at 13, I taught myself Python; and by 15, I enrolled in a technical high school. I quickly realized that what fascinated me most was algorithms, more than web development or game creation. I experimented for a while—robotics, development, Arduino projects—before finding a true calling in artificial intelligence.
about.section1p2: That spark came during an AI internship at Cali Intelligences, a computer-vision startup. It was a revelation: I threw myself into it with one conviction — to contribute to the foundations of AI by rethinking the model architectures themselves (bio-inspiration, world models, efficiency) rather than stacking more layers. That conviction led me to the ENSEIRB-MATMECA engineer-doctor track, and then to research at the University of Alberta's Vision & Learning Lab.
about.section2Title: A Determination to Pivot and Excel
about.section2p1: Coming from a practice-oriented background, I made the demanding choice to pivot toward academia to strengthen my foundations in mathematics and algorithms. I was admitted to ENSEIRB-MATMECA on merit and ranked among the top 3 of my class, proving that determination and self-teaching can rival the most traditional paths.

education.subtitle: Because in deep learning, there’s also supervised learning. But unlike machines, I sought knowledge—not just validation.
education.footer: Because learning isn’t just about optimizing parameters, it’s about giving meaning to every iteration.
